title: Quick Start
text: framedf first-pass diagnostic unfamiliar data frame. tries read data way careful colleague — figuring column means, noticing pairs columns move together, flagging values look wrong. result qualitative. get sentences like “temperature decreases strongly elevation”, Pearson coefficients. Numeric evidence still object anyone wants ; printed view stays calm. one entry point, frame(), four reader functions: print(frame(df)) — narrative overview. relationships(frame(df)) — pairs grouped meaningful, suspicious, structural, ignored. anomalies(frame(df)) — per-column oddities. details(frame(df)) — methods, roles, skipped rules, backend.

title: Quick Start
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("gcol33/framedf")

title: Quick Start
text: four sections print output map onto four parts first-look conversation: Structure — kind dataframe ? shaped, identifies row, temporal/spatial axis? Relationships — moves ? patterns real feel like artefacts? Anomalies — looks wrong? Ignored — excluded screening, ?
code: set.seed(1) n <- 300L df <- data.frame(   plot_id    = sample(1:60, n, replace = TRUE),   year       = sample(2010:2020, n, replace = TRUE),   latitude   = stats::runif(n, 40, 50),   longitude  = stats::runif(n, 5, 15),   elevation  = stats::runif(n, 0, 2500),   observer   = sample(letters[1:6], n, replace = TRUE),   country    = sample(c("AT", "DE", "CH"), n, replace = TRUE),   richness   = NA_real_,   cover_a    = stats::runif(n, 0, 1),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) df$temperature <- 20 - df$elevation / 200 + stats::rnorm(n) df$richness    <- 5 + 0.4 * sqrt(stats::runif(n, 1, 100)) +                   2 * (df$observer == "a") + stats::rnorm(n) df$cover_b     <- 1 - df$cover_a fd <- frame(df) print(fd) #> framedf #>  #> 300 rows × 11 columns #>  #> Structure #> ──────────────── #> Looks like a repeated-measure observational dataframe. #>  #> Detected temporal structure: #> • year #>  #> Detected spatial structure: #> • latitude #> • longitude #>  #> Likely identifiers: #> • plot_id #>  #> Possible grouping structure: #> • repeated observations within plot_id #> • observations grouped by country #>  #>  #> Relationships #> ──────────────── #> temperature strongly decreases with elevation #> cover_b strongly decreases with cover_a #> observer identity appears to influence richness estimates #> plot_id identity appears to influence elevation estimates #>   possible observer effect #> plot_id identity appears to influence richness estimates #>   possible observer effect #> plot_id identity appears to influence cover_a estimates #>   possible observer effect #> plot_id identity appears to influence temperature estimates #>   possible observer effect #> plot_id identity appears to influence cover_b estimates #>   possible observer effect #> cover_a and cover_b behave as constrained complements #>  #> Anomalies #> ──────────────── #> richness contains a noticeable number of outliers #>  #> Ignored relationships #> ──────────────── #> plot_id was ignored because it behaves like a grouping identifier #> year was ignored because it is a temporal column, screened separately as drift #> latitude was ignored because it is a spatial coordinate, screened separately as drift #> longitude was ignored because it is a spatial coordinate, screened separately as drift

title: Quick Start
text: details() place go output surprises . tells mode screening ran , role column given, rules caused pairs skipped, whether C++ backend used.
code: relationships(fd) #> Relationships #>  #> meaningful #> ──────────────── #> temperature ~ elevation #>   direction: negative #>   strength: strong #>   stability: high #>   method: numeric screening #>  #> cover_b ~ cover_a #>   direction: negative #>   strength: strong #>   stability: high #>   method: numeric screening #>  #> richness ~ observer #>   pattern: group effect #>   strength: strong #>   stability: high #>   method: categorical numeric screen #>  #> suspicious #> ──────────────── #> elevation ~ plot_id #>   pattern: group effect #>   strength: moderate #>   stability: high #>   method: categorical numeric screen #>   concern: possible observer effect #>  #> richness ~ plot_id #>   pattern: group effect #>   strength: moderate #>   stability: high #>   method: categorical numeric screen #>   concern: possible observer effect #>  #> cover_a ~ plot_id #>   pattern: group effect #>   strength: moderate #>   stability: high #>   method: categorical numeric screen #>   concern: possible observer effect #>  #> temperature ~ plot_id #>   pattern: group effect #>   strength: moderate #>   stability: high #>   method: categorical numeric screen #>   concern: possible observer effect #>  #> cover_b ~ plot_id #>   pattern: group effect #>   strength: moderate #>   stability: high #>   method: categorical numeric screen #>   concern: possible observer effect #>  #> structural #> ──────────────── #> cover_b ~ cover_a #>   pattern: constrained complement #>   concern: compositional relationship #>   method: compositional sum check #>  #> ignored #> ──────────────── #> plot_id ~ elevation #>   reason: it behaves like a grouping identifier #>  #> year ~ elevation #>   reason: it is a temporal column, screened separately as drift #>  #> latitude ~ elevation #>   reason: it is a spatial coordinate, screened separately as drift #>  #> longitude ~ elevation #>   reason: it is a spatial coordinate, screened separately as drift anomalies(fd) #> Anomalies #>  #> outliers #> ──────────────── #> richness #>   pattern: outliers (Tukey fence) #>   count: 3 #>   fence: [3.43923, 12.6733] details(fd) #> Details #>  #> Analysis mode #> ──────────────── #> relationship screening used the full data #> all pairs above the minimum complete-case count were screened directly #>  #> Column roles #> ──────────────── #> plot_id: grouping identifier #> year: temporal #> latitude: spatial coordinate (latitude) #> longitude: spatial coordinate (longitude) #> elevation: continuous #> observer: categorical #> country: categorical #> richness: continuous #> cover_a: compositional #> temperature: continuous #> cover_b: compositional #>  #> Skipped relationship rules #> ──────────────── #> identifier × anything: skipped #> administrative index × anything: skipped #> near-constant or constant × anything: skipped #> temporal × measurement: not screened symmetrically; checked as drift instead #> coordinate × measurement: not screened symmetrically; checked as drift instead #> many-level grouping × continuous: screened, but flagged as observer-style if strong #>  #> Backend #> ──────────────── #> primitives: C++ (Rcpp) #> numeric numeric pairs: screened by ordinary least squares (with QR residualisation when adjusted) #> categorical numeric pairs: one-way analysis-of-variance summaries, eta squared as effect size #> compositional pairs: pairwise sum stability under coefficient of variation #> drift checks: simple linear fits of spatial coordinates against time

title: Quick Start
text: suspect one variable confounding others, can partial screening:
code: fd_adj <- frame(df, adjustment = "elevation") relationships(fd_adj, kind = "meaningful") #> Relationships #>  #> meaningful #> ──────────────── #> cover_b ~ cover_a #>   direction: negative #>   strength: strong #>   stability: high #>   method: numeric screening (adjusted) #>  #> richness ~ observer #>   pattern: group effect #>   strength: strong #>   stability: high #>   method: categorical numeric screen

title: Quick Start
text: Everything configurable framedf_settings() ... frame():
code: frame(df,       strong_threshold   = 0.6,       moderate_threshold = 0.4,       weak_threshold     = 0.15)

title: Quick Start
text: Workflows — practical examples (large data, repeated measures, observer effects). Roles rules — role means columns excluded screening.

title: Roles and Rules
text: correlation matrix treats row_id temperature identically. numeric, . conceptually column — row_id bookkeeping, strong correlation row_id temperature almost always sign data sorted time, real association. Role inference lets framedf say “latitude ignored spatial coordinate, screened separately drift” instead just dumping Pearson coefficient.

title: Roles and Rules
text: Column roles block details() lists every column inferred role. Anything Ignored section print(fd) also .
code: df <- data.frame(   PlotID            = sample(1:50, 200, replace = TRUE),   PlotObservationID = seq_len(200L),   row_number        = seq_len(200L),   year              = sample(1990:2020, 200, replace = TRUE),   latitude          = stats::runif(200, 40, 50),   longitude         = stats::runif(200, 5, 15),   elevation         = stats::runif(200, 0, 2500),   observer_id       = sample(letters[1:5], 200, replace = TRUE),   country           = sample(c("AT", "DE", "CH"), 200, replace = TRUE),   native_cover      = stats::runif(200, 0, 1),   flagged           = sample(c(TRUE, FALSE), 200, replace = TRUE),   stringsAsFactors  = FALSE ) df$alien_cover <- 1 - df$native_cover fd <- frame(df) details(fd) #> Details #>  #> Analysis mode #> ──────────────── #> relationship screening used the full data #> all pairs above the minimum complete-case count were screened directly #>  #> Column roles #> ──────────────── #> PlotID: grouping identifier #> PlotObservationID: identifier #> row_number: administrative index #> year: temporal #> latitude: spatial coordinate (latitude) #> longitude: spatial coordinate (longitude) #> elevation: continuous #> observer_id: grouping identifier #> country: categorical #> native_cover: compositional #> flagged: logical flag #> alien_cover: compositional #>  #> Skipped relationship rules #> ──────────────── #> identifier × anything: skipped #> administrative index × anything: skipped #> near-constant or constant × anything: skipped #> temporal × measurement: not screened symmetrically; checked as drift instead #> coordinate × measurement: not screened symmetrically; checked as drift instead #> many-level grouping × continuous: screened, but flagged as observer-style if strong #>  #> Backend #> ──────────────── #> primitives: C++ (Rcpp) #> numeric numeric pairs: screened by ordinary least squares (with QR residualisation when adjusted) #> categorical numeric pairs: one-way analysis-of-variance summaries, eta squared as effect size #> compositional pairs: pairwise sum stability under coefficient of variation #> drift checks: simple linear fits of spatial coordinates against time

title: Roles and Rules
text: Every threshold parameter: framedf_settings() returns full default list documentation every field.
code: frame(df,       id_unique_ratio     = 0.99,   # require 99% unique to call a string an id       near_constant_ratio = 0.01,   # call near-constant if dominant > 99%       strong_threshold    = 0.6,       moderate_threshold  = 0.4,       weak_threshold      = 0.15,       compositional_cv    = 0.05,       observer_min_levels = 4L,       min_level_n         = 5L)

title: Roles and Rules
text: Role inference heuristic, edge cases land wrong bucket sometimes. Two ways recover: Rename column. Names like *_id, *_year, *_lat, *_cover picked immediately. Read role fd$roles override analysis manually: triage object plain list, post-hoc edits safe. production use, prefer fixing column name extending package’s name-based heuristics.
code: fd <- frame(df) fd$roles[["my_weird_column"]] <- "measurement" # (re-run any reader you need on the modified object)

title: Workflows
text: Goal. dataset plant richness measurements multiple observers. want know whether observer identity influences recorded richness ecology . Challenge. Standard correlation matrices ignore categorical variables, observer dimension invisible diagnostic tools. Strategy. framedf runs one-way ANOVA every categorical column every measurement column. categorical column identifier-like group effect large, pair reclassified suspicious concern “possible observer effect”. screen flags richness ~ observer_id group effect possible observer-style concern. Note meaningful richness ~ plot_area relationship still present "meaningful" block; suspicious flag erase .
code: set.seed(1) n <- 400L obs   <- sample(letters[1:8], n, replace = TRUE) bias  <- stats::setNames(stats::rnorm(8, 0, 4), letters[1:8]) df <- data.frame(   observer_id = obs,   plot_area   = stats::runif(n, 1, 100),   richness    = NA_real_,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) df$richness <- 10 + 0.3 * sqrt(df$plot_area) + bias[df$observer_id] +                stats::rnorm(n) fd <- frame(df) relationships(fd, kind = "suspicious") #> Relationships #>  #> suspicious #> ──────────────── #> richness ~ observer_id #>   pattern: group effect #>   strength: strong #>   stability: high #>   method: categorical numeric screen #>   concern: possible observer effect relationships(fd, kind = "meaningful") #> Relationships #> No relationships meet the criteria.

title: Workflows
text: Goal. long-running monitoring program year column latitude / longitude columns. want know whether geographic sampling shifted time. Challenge. Time space appear many tables rarely checked together — yet slow drift common source confounded trend estimates. Strategy. framedf runs dedicated drift screen every temporal × spatial pair. slope moderate strong, pair reported suspicious concern “sampling design may spatially structured”. drift year longitude surfaced suspicious. Plain numeric pair screening hidden — year longitude excluded symmetric numeric sweep role-tagged temporal spatial respectively.
code: set.seed(2) yr <- sample(2000:2020, 500, replace = TRUE) df_drift <- data.frame(   year      = yr,   latitude  = stats::runif(500, 40, 50),   longitude = 10 + (yr - 2010) * 0.3 + stats::rnorm(500, sd = 0.1),   richness  = stats::rnorm(500, 30) ) fd_drift <- frame(df_drift) relationships(fd_drift, kind = "suspicious") #> Relationships #>  #> suspicious #> ──────────────── #> longitude ~ year #>   pattern: temporal spatial drift #>   strength: strong #>   stability: high #>   method: drift screen #>   concern: sampling design may be spatially structured

title: Workflows
text: Goal. Two cover variables (native cover alien cover) recorded fractions plot. want make sure package treats constrained pair, independent measurements. Challenge. naive correlation two mechanically strong hijack meaningful section. Strategy. Compositional pairs whose pairwise sum approximately constant routed separate structural block. still visible — user can decide — mixed genuine ecological signals. native_cover ~ alien_cover pair shows structural — described constrained complement — genuine richness ~ temperature association lives meaningful.
code: set.seed(3) n <- 200L df_comp <- data.frame(   native_cover = stats::runif(n, 0, 1),   richness     = stats::rnorm(n, 30) ) df_comp$alien_cover  <- 1 - df_comp$native_cover df_comp$temperature  <- 20 - 0.01 * df_comp$richness + stats::rnorm(n) fd_comp <- frame(df_comp) relationships(fd_comp) #> Relationships #>  #> meaningful #> ──────────────── #> alien_cover ~ native_cover #>   direction: negative #>   strength: strong #>   stability: high #>   method: numeric screening #>  #> structural #> ──────────────── #> alien_cover ~ native_cover #>   pattern: constrained complement #>   concern: compositional relationship #>   method: compositional sum check

title: Workflows
text: Goal. million-row table big screen fully every pair. want sensible relationships seconds, minutes. Challenge. Naive pair screening millions rows multiplies many regressions. Strategy. subsample_threshold rows (default 50 000), framedf runs small probe sample first drops obviously weak pairs. Surviving pairs confirmed larger sample. Stability recorded per pair: high passes agree tier, medium disagree, low probe alone carried. details() report “relationship screening used progressive subsampling” whenever input threshold.
code: fd_big <- frame(big_df) details(fd_big)

title: Contribution Guidelines
text: reporting issue, please include: - minimal reproducible example (reprex) - Output sessionInfo() - Expected vs. actual results - Threshold method used (e.g., "els" "bron-kerbosch") - R operating system version - Toolchain info relevant (e.g., Rtools Windows) contributing framedf, agree code released license package.

what: framedf: Calm Triage of Unfamiliar Data Frames
title: framedf: Calm Triage of Unfamiliar Data Frames
text: Calm triage unfamiliar data frames. framedf reads data frame way experienced analyst first thirty seconds: infers column means, screens every sensible pair relationships, lists anomalies worth reading first. output qualitative — direction, strength, stability — raw test statistics.

title: framedf: Calm Triage of Unfamiliar Data Frames
code: library(framedf)  set.seed(1) df <- data.frame(   plot_id    = sample(1:40, 200, replace = TRUE),   year       = sample(2010:2020, 200, replace = TRUE),   latitude   = runif(200, 40, 50),   longitude  = runif(200, 5, 15),   elevation  = runif(200, 0, 2500),   richness   = rpois(200, 30) ) df$temperature <- 20 - df$elevation / 200 + rnorm(200)  print(frame(df)) framedf  200 rows × 7 columns  Structure ──────────────── Looks like a spatially and temporally indexed observational dataframe.  Detected temporal structure: • year  Detected spatial structure: • longitude • latitude  Likely identifiers: • plot_id  Relationships ──────────────── temperature strongly decreases with elevation  Anomalies ──────────────── No anomalies detected by the default checks.  Ignored relationships ──────────────── plot_id was ignored because it behaves like a grouping identifier year was ignored because it is a temporal column, screened separately as drift longitude was ignored because it is a spatial coordinate, screened separately as drift latitude was ignored because it is a spatial coordinate, screened separately as drift

title: framedf: Calm Triage of Unfamiliar Data Frames
text: first task new dataset : figure column , pairs columns covary, values look wrong. Existing tools either produce dense statistical dumps (raw correlation matrices, autoreport tables) shallow summaries (str(), summary()). Neither reads like colleague telling noticed. framedf built conversational first pass. infers semantic role column — identifier, temporal, spatial, categorical, continuous, compositional, near-constant — uses decide pairs screen; groups relationship findings meaningful (real signal measurements), suspicious (observer-like effects, sampling drift), structural (compositional complements); surfaces anomalies qualitative patterns: implausible ranges, distributional outliers, inconsistent capitalisation, totals exceeding bounds; scales millions rows two-stage progressive subsampling numeric pairs. model fit. read--data-pass happen modelling.

title: framedf: Calm Triage of Unfamiliar Data Frames
text: frame() — build triage object data frame. print(frame(df)) — narrative overview Structure, Relationships, Anomalies, Ignored sections. relationships() — meaningful, suspicious, structural, ignored pairs direction, strength, stability. anomalies() — per-column oddities grouped qualitative pattern. details() — analysis mode, column roles, skipped rules, backend used. framedf_settings() — every threshold tunable.

title: framedf: Calm Triage of Unfamiliar Data Frames
text: Roles: identifier, administrative index, grouping identifier, temporal, latitude, longitude, continuous measurement, compositional (cover, share, percent), categorical, logical flag, near-constant, constant. Relationships: linear association (optional adjustment), one-way ANOVA-style group effects, temporal × spatial drift, constrained-complement compositional pairs. Anomalies: Tukey-fence outliers, moment-based skew, implausible coordinate ranges, totals exceeding bounds, inconsistent capitalisation, rare categorical levels.

title: framedf: Calm Triage of Unfamiliar Data Frames
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("gcol33/framedf")

title: framedf: Calm Triage of Unfamiliar Data Frames
text: adjustment supplied, every numeric–numeric screen run residualised values (QR-based partial-), confounded pairs longer appear meaningful.
code: fd <- frame(df, adjustment = "elevation") relationships(fd)

title: framedf: Calm Triage of Unfamiliar Data Frames
code: fd <- frame(df,             strong_threshold   = 0.6,             moderate_threshold = 0.4,             weak_threshold     = 0.15)

title: framedf: Calm Triage of Unfamiliar Data Frames
code: fd <- frame(df) print(fd)          # narrative overview relationships(fd)  # ordered by kind anomalies(fd)      # ordered by pattern details(fd)        # how the analysis was done

title: framedf: Calm Triage of Unfamiliar Data Frames
text: Numeric × numeric — ordinary least squares, QR residualisation adjustment set. data frames larger subsample_threshold (default 50 000 rows) framedf runs small probe sample first, drops weak pairs, re-screens survivors larger confirmation sample. Stability reported high, medium, low depending whether two passes agree. Categorical × numeric — one-way analysis variance. eta squared effect size becomes strength tier. categorical column many levels, looks like identifier, shows strong group effect, pair reclassified suspicious (possible observer effect). Temporal × spatial — separate drift screen looks sampling structure spatial coordinate moves systematically time. Compositional pairs — pairs cover-like columns whose sum coefficient variation compositional_cv marked structural. Identifier-like, near-constant, date columns excluded symmetric pair sweep listed Ignored section one-line reason. C++ primitives (Rcpp) handle inner loops; R layer one read.

what: Triage a Data Frame for Structure, Relationships, and Anomalies — frame
title: Triage a Data Frame for Structure, Relationships, and Anomalies — frame
text: frame() reads data frame, infers semantic role column (identifier, temporal, spatial, categorical, continuous, ...), screens sensible pairs columns relationships, surfaces small set anomalies single pass. result calm, qualitative summary suitable first thirty seconds working unfamiliar dataset, model fit.

title: Triage a Data Frame for Structure, Relationships, and Anomalies — frame

title: Triage a Data Frame for Structure, Relationships, and Anomalies — frame
text: data data frame. adjustment Optional character vector column names partial (residualize ) numeric–numeric screening. Useful one variable suspected confounding pairs. ... Optional overrides screening settings. See framedf_settings() full list defaults.

title: Triage a Data Frame for Structure, Relationships, and Anomalies — frame
text: object class "frame_df" containing role assignments, structure summary, relationship findings, ignored pairs, anomaly findings, settings used.

title: Triage a Data Frame for Structure, Relationships, and Anomalies — frame
text: Three reader functions consume result: print.frame_df() — narrative overview (default print(frame(df))) relationships() — meaningful, suspicious, structural, ignored pairs anomalies()      — per-column oddities (range, distribution, capitalization) details()        — analysis mode, column roles, skipped rules, backend

title: Triage a Data Frame for Structure, Relationships, and Anomalies — frame
code: set.seed(1) n <- 200 df <- data.frame(   plot_id    = sample(1:40, n, replace = TRUE),   year       = sample(2010:2020, n, replace = TRUE),   latitude   = runif(n, 40, 50),   longitude  = runif(n, 5, 15),   elevation  = runif(n, 0, 2500),   temperature = NA_real_,   richness   = rpois(n, 30),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) df$temperature <- 20 - df$elevation / 200 + stats::rnorm(n) fd <- frame(df) print(fd)

title: framedf: Calm Triage of Unfamiliar Data Frames — framedf-package
text: framedf looks data frame way experienced analyst first thirty seconds: infers column means, screens every sensible pair relationships, lists anomalies worth reading first. output qualitative — direction, strength, stability — raw test statistics.

title: framedf: Calm Triage of Unfamiliar Data Frames — framedf-package
text: frame() build triage object data frame. print.frame_df() narrative overview. relationships() meaningful, suspicious, structural, ignored pairs. anomalies() per-column oddities. details() analysis mode, roles, skipped rules, backend. framedf_settings() tunable thresholds.

text: adjustment Optional adjustment columns (passed unchanged). ... Named overrides.

title: Settings for frame() — framedf_settings
text: named list settings.

title: Settings for frame() — framedf_settings
text: min_obs Minimum complete cases required screen pair. strong_threshold, moderate_threshold, weak_threshold Absolute correlation cut-offs used classify pair strength. near_constant_ratio Maximum allowed share dominant value column flagged near-constant. id_unique_ratio character column share unique values, treated identifier. skew_threshold Absolute skewness column flagged right- left-skewed. subsample_threshold Row count numeric pairs screened progressive subsampling. subsample_probe, subsample_confirm Probe confirmation sample sizes two-stage subsampling. compositional_cv Maximum coefficient variation x + y pair flagged constrained complement. observer_min_levels Minimum number categorical levels strong group effect counts observer-style concern. min_level_n Levels fewer many rows flagged rare. seed Seed used subsampling layer. adjustment Optional column names partial numeric–numeric screening.

title: Print a frame_df Object — print.frame_df
text: Prints calm, qualitative narrative data frame. output organised four sections: Structure, Relationships, Anomalies, Ignored. raw correlation values, p-values, test statistics shown – live relationships() details() readers want .

title: Show Relationships Found by frame() — relationships
code: set.seed(1) df <- data.frame(   x = rnorm(100),   y = rnorm(100) ) df$z <- 0.8 * df$x + 0.2 * stats::rnorm(100) relationships(frame(df))

title: framedf 0.1.0
text: Initial public release.

title: framedf 0.1.0
text: frame() builds single triage object data frame, infers semantic role every column, screens sensible pairs columns, lists anomalies one pass. Roles: id, admin_index, group_id, temporal, coord_lat, coord_lon, measurement, compositional, categorical, flag, near_constant, constant, unknown.

title: framedf 0.1.0
text: print.frame_df() — narrative overview Structure, Relationships, Anomalies, Ignored sections. Output qualitative; raw test statistics default view. relationships() — relationships grouped meaningful, suspicious, structural, ignored direction, strength, stability, method per pair. anomalies() — per-column oddities grouped pattern. details() — analysis mode, column roles, skipped rules, backend.

title: framedf 0.1.0
text: Numeric × numeric: simple OLS, optionally residualised adjustment set via adjustment = c(...). Two-stage progressive subsampling large data: small probe sample drops obviously weak pairs, surviving pairs confirmed larger sample. Stability tier (high / medium / low) reflects agreement across passes. Categorical × numeric: one-way analysis--variance summaries eta squared. Strong group effects identifier-like categoricals flagged suspicious (possible observer effect). Temporal × spatial drift: dedicated check surfaces sampling designs coordinates move systematically time. Compositional pairs: cover-like columns whose pairwise sum stable flagged structural (constrained complement).

title: framedf 0.1.0
text: Tukey-fence outliers moment-based skew continuous columns. Implausible coordinate ranges (latitude / longitude bounds). Compositional totals exceed inferred 0–1 0–100 scale. Inconsistent capitalisation categorical columns. rare categorical levels (configurable via min_level_n).

title: framedf 0.1.0
text: framedf_settings() exposes every threshold: min_obs, strong_threshold, moderate_threshold, weak_threshold, near_constant_ratio, id_unique_ratio, skew_threshold, subsample_threshold, subsample_probe, subsample_confirm, compositional_cv, observer_min_levels, min_level_n, seed.

title: framedf 0.1.0
text: C++ primitives (Rcpp) lm_simple, residualize, group_summary. Falls back pure-R implementations shared library loaded.
